newsSince: No material new company event in the Jun 9-23 window. Q1 results were 1 Jun (before the window); CEO/CFO held a Wells Fargo Industrials fireside on 11 Jun with no fresh disclosure. The standing tailwind is favorable activated-carbon mix as hybrid-vehicle demand rises post-EV-credit expiry; FY26 EPS guidance (~$4.80-5.20, adjusted for the Road Markings divestiture) is unchanged.

sources.label: Ingevity reaffirms FY26 EPS, advances APT sale (Seeking Alpha)
Ingevity CEO/CFO at Wells Fargo Industrials conf (BusinessWire, 9 Jun 2026)
Ingevity execs at 2026 Wells Fargo Industrials conference (Jun 9/11)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material company-specific news since Jun 9 (Q1 was 1 Jun; 11 Jun fireside added nothing). The strict-screen latent thesis — activated carbon/specialty materials as a possible filtration/adsorption/battery-adjacent node — is unchanged; hybrid-vehicle carbon mix is a steady tailwind, not an AI-node inflection. Hold at corrected rank.
verdicts.bull: Specialty activated-carbon franchise with hybrid-vehicle mix tailwind; optionality into filtration/adsorption niches.
verdicts.neutral: AI/data-center linkage is speculative and latent; story is auto-emissions and chemicals, not compute.
verdicts.bear: Core demand tied to autos/emissions regs; +21% YTD with thin direct AI relevance.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A concrete data-center/electronics filtration or battery-adjacent design win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto-cycle softness; EV-vs-hybrid mix shift erodes carbon volumes.
